title: mentha austriaca var. multiflora
intro: Mentha austriaca var. multiflora, also known as Austrian peppermint, is a subspecies of mint native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its numerous small flowers and distinctive minty aroma. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and digestive properties, which are attributed to its high content of menthol and other essential oils. The health benefits of Mentha austriaca var. multiflora include relief from digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to freshen breath and reduce stress. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, tincture, or essential oil for its medicinal and therapeutic uses.
